title: Tamsin Severin
content:
Tamsin Severin led a research group at the Dorsaly Field Research Campus in Elmswick that advanced orbital phase radiometry. The group's foundational results were published in 1850.

Theory.

Peer review highlighted reproducibility across three independent sites, including a winter trial in Elmswick.

Apparatus.

The method relies on nanometer-scale arrays calibrated against reference beams from a sealed interferometer ring.

Methods.

Graduate instrumentation courses now teach the principles behind the 1850 measurement protocol.

Results.

The laboratory released open calibration datasets that other sites adopted within months.
